claim: Running 4 parallel Claude Code windows with mixed effort levels (high, medium, low) allows parallelization of AI coding work while keeping mental load manageable; typical developer capacity is 4 parallel workstreams
actionable: Launch 4 parallel Claude Code instances with different effort levels for different tasks: high-effort for complex work, low-effort for quick information gathering
source_title: The Agentic Engineer Workflow You Need In 2026

claim: Using /compact and /clear commands to reset context windows prevents context drift, which degrades AI reasoning quality; timing for reset depends on project complexity with no one-size-fits-all answer
actionable: Monitor context window size during development and use /compact or /clear when context approaches limits to prevent drift in AI responses
source_title: The Agentic Engineer Workflow You Need In 2026

claim: Spec-driven development, BMAD method, and prompt engineering trends are gimmicks that don't survive 6 months; only principles that have stood the test of time should be adopted
actionable: Focus on engineering fundamentals from established books rather than chasing new methodologies; adopt techniques only if they'll still be relevant in 6 months
source_title: The Agentic Engineer Workflow You Need In 2026

claim: The 'smell' command pattern references Clean Code and design patterns books to push AI code toward higher quality; this is more effective than generic prompts saying 'you are a senior engineer'
actionable: Create a skill/command that references specific engineering books (Clean Code, Design Patterns) to guide AI code quality rather than generic senior engineer prompts
source_title: The Agentic Engineer Workflow You Need In 2026

claim: Git worktrees allow parallel AI coding in independent workstreams without merge conflicts, even on the same branch; each worktree is a temporary copy that can be deleted after use
actionable: Use git worktrees when running multiple Claude Code agents on parallel tasks to avoid branch conflicts; clean up worktrees after completion
source_title: The Agentic Engineer Workflow You Need In 2026

claim: Realistic productivity gains from AI coding are 30-60%, not 5x; people claiming 5x productivity are often not accountable for code quality in real team environments
actionable: Set expectations for AI coding productivity at 30-60% gains in team environments, not 5x; measure gains in reduced code review time and faster feature shipping
source_title: The Agentic Engineer Workflow You Need In 2026

claim: MCP servers excel for specialized tools (e.g., Context7 for pulling latest docs with filtering) vs generic web search; Bash tools are better for simple CLIs like GitHub; choose based on operation complexity
actionable: Use MCP servers for specialized knowledge retrieval, Bash for simple CLI operations; avoid MCP for single-command operations where Bash suffices
source_title: The Agentic Engineer Workflow You Need In 2026
